title: Sidekick icon to replace profile picture in Activity Logs
excerpt:
Description
For any task creations, deletions, or changes that happen by Sidekick, I would like to see a Sidekick icon next to the record in the Activity Logs instead of the user who is prompting Sidekick. Or, if I could…

posts.author: beth1021 (Elisabeth Juliano)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

For any task creations, deletions, or changes that happen by Sidekick, I would like to see a Sidekick icon next to the record in the Activity Logs instead of the user who is prompting Sidekick. Or, if I could see both, that would be even better.

What are you trying to achieve

We have had multiple instances where a team member is using Sidekick, it creates items for them, but then also deletes items if Sidekick feels it matches the prompt. The team member then gets confused on where certain items have gone. When looking at the Activity Logs, it shows the user as the one who deleted the items, but the user didn’t manually decide to do so. It was during the timeframe that they were using Sidekick. We should be able to tell if AI is creating or deleting items versus the user manually doing it.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Elisabeth Juliano:

ems for them, but then also deletes items if Sidekick feels it matches the prompt. The team member then gets confused on where certain items have gone. When looking at the Activity Logs, it shows the user as the one who deleted the items, but the user didn’t manually decide to do so. It was during the timeframe that they were using Sidekick. We should be able to tell if AI is creating or deleting items versus the user manually doing it.

Completely agree with this.

Right now it’s really hard to tell the difference between something a user actually did vs something Sidekick did on their behalf, especially when it comes to creating or deleting items.

From an audit and troubleshooting standpoint, that distinction matters a lot.

Even just a simple indicator like “Sidekick (via User)” or a separate icon in the activity log would make a huge difference in clarity and trust.

Feels like a pretty important gap as AI starts taking more actions inside boards
